description: Test 'PROP-EDIT-003: lastSaveAt === lastEditAt (saved exactly at edit time)' has the assertion body `expect(result).toBeDefined();` — it does not assert any specific value. The test docstring even acknowledges 'implementation decides; test records the expected behavior per spec' but then fails to encode the spec decision. The current implementation (debounceSchedule.ts:50) returns `{ shouldFire: false, fireAt: null }` for `lastSaveAt >= lastEditAt && lastSaveAt !== 0`, which is sensible but unverified. The boundary `lastSaveAt === lastEditAt` could regress without test failure.
suggestedRemediation: Replace `expect(result).toBeDefined()` with `expect(result).toEqual({ shouldFire: false, fireAt: null })` (matching current implementation) or update the spec to document the boundary. Either way, the test must encode the canonical behavior so a future regression is caught.
